overview: Provides medical, sickness, maternity, disablement, dependant and other statutory social security benefits to eligible employees through the Employees' State Insurance Scheme administered by ESIC. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

definitions.definition: An employee covered under the Employees' State Insurance Act and the applicable scheme.
The establishment or person responsible for compliance under the Act.
An employee registered under the Employees' State Insurance Scheme.

rules.title: Review ESI Applicability
rules.description: Determine whether the establishment is required to register under the Employees' State Insurance Scheme based on applicable legal criteria and notified coverage. :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}

rules.title: Employer Registration
rules.description: Register the establishment with ESIC where coverage applies. :contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2}

rules.title: Employee Registration
rules.description: Register eligible employees under the Employees' State Insurance Scheme. :contentReference[oaicite:3]{index=3}

rules.title: Monthly ESI Contributions
rules.description: Deposit employer and employee contributions within the prescribed timelines at the applicable contribution rates. :contentReference[oaicite:4]{index=4}

rules.title: Maintain ESI Records
rules.description: Maintain statutory registers, wage records and contribution records required under the scheme. :contentReference[oaicite:5]{index=5}

rules.title: Facilitate Statutory Benefits
rules.description: Ensure eligible employees are able to access statutory medical and cash benefits available under the ESI Scheme. :contentReference[oaicite:6]{index=6}

exceptions.title: Coverage depends on notified applicability
exceptions.description: Applicability varies based on notified establishments, employee coverage criteria and other legal provisions. The engine should evaluate these before recommending compliance actions. :contentReference[oaicite:7]{index=7}
